Controller

Austin, Texas|Direct Hire|Accounting|Posted 1 year ago

Job Description

Job Summary:

The Controller will be responsible for overseeing all financial and accounting activities within the company. This role ensures the accuracy and integrity of financial statements, implements internal controls, and manages financial reporting processes. The Controller will work closely with the CFO and other senior management to provide strategic financial guidance, analyze business performance, and drive financial initiatives that align with the company's long-term objectives.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Oversee the daily operations of the accounting department, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, and general ledger functions.
  • Prepare and review monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in compliance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP).
  • Ensure accuracy and integrity of financial data, including reviewing accounting transactions and preparing reconciliations.
  • Manage and oversee the company’s budgeting process, including the preparation of forecasts, variance analysis, and cost control measures.
  • Develop and implement internal controls to ensure the accuracy and reliability of financial reporting.
  • Coordinate and lead the annual audit process, liaising with external auditors and ensuring timely submission of audited financial statements.
  • Provide financial analysis and guidance to senior management on key business decisions, profitability, and performance metrics.
  • Monitor cash flow, investment strategies, and financing activities to ensure liquidity and financial stability.
  • Oversee tax compliance and ensure timely submission of tax returns and filings.
  • Evaluate and enhance financial systems, processes, and procedures to improve efficiency and accuracy.
  • Ensure compliance with all applicable financial regulations and company policies.
  • Provide leadership and mentorship to accounting team members, fostering a collaborative and high-performance environment.
  • Assist in preparing reports and presentations for the Board of Directors, investors, and other stakeholders.

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • CPA (Certified Public Accountant) or equivalent professional certification is highly preferred.
  • Minimum of [X] years of experience in accounting or finance, with at least [X] years in a leadership or managerial role.
  • Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial regulations, and financial reporting standards.
  • Proficiency with accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, NetSuite, SAP) and Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
  • Experience with financial forecasting, budgeting, and variance analysis.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex financial data.
  • Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
  • Ability to communicate financial information to both financial and non-financial stakeholders.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and work within tight deadlines.
  • Strong leadership and team management skills.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Master’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration.
  • Previous experience in [industry-specific knowledge or experience].
  • Knowledge of international accounting standards and practices, if applicable.
  • Experience with mergers, acquisitions, and other corporate financial activities.
  • Familiarity with tax planning and strategic financial management.

Why Join Us:

  • Opportunity to be part of a growing and dynamic organization.
  • Collaborative, fast-paced work environment.
  • Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
  • Professional development opportunities and career growth.
  • [Additional perks or company-specific benefits, such as flexible working hours, health benefits, etc.]
